title: jit apply leaves a dangling doc reference: scaffolded bracket fails validation until the file is hand-created
description:
Eager validation fails a freshly-applied bracket before the lifecycle stage that produces what it checks has happened. Two coupled mechanisms: a not-yet-authored plan document is treated as a hard error, and `coverage-preview` fires before breakdown starts. Applying a bracket should leave the repository valid, with each check enforcing only once its subject is due.

## Background

Applying the `plan` template (`jit apply plan <container>`) scaffolds a planning node `P` and a breakdown node `B`. Two validation paths then fail a bracket that is correctly mid-lifecycle:

1. **Plan document.** `P`'s `doc` field names a plan-doc path (e.g. `dev/active/<container.id>-plan.md`). Apply also attached a `plan`-labeled document reference to that not-yet-created file, and validation re-resolved the path repo-wide and read it, so `jit validate` reported a hard error the instant the bracket was applied — blocking unrelated transitions until a file was hand-created. The plan is `P`'s deliverable; it does not exist until `P` is worked.

2. **Coverage preview.** `coverage-preview` fired the moment `B` existed (`when = { type = "breakdown" }`), asserting every `[hard]` criterion of the container is satisfied by a child. A freshly-applied `B` is in backlog with no children, so every criterion read as uncovered — coverage cannot exist before breakdown has produced anything.

Both observed live on epic `f2532a2d`.

## Resolution

- **No link, instruct instead.** Apply attaches no document reference; the planning node's `doc` location is seeded into its description as an instruction to author the plan there and link it once written. The author links it during planning.
- **Plan-doc presence is gated on the planning node.** A missing external plan is acceptable while `P` is not `done` (skipped, no error); once `P` is `done` the plan must exist (downstream coverage reads it) and a still-missing file is an error. Other read failures always error.
- **Coverage preview is gated on the breakdown node.** It enforces only when `B` is `in_progress`, `gated`, or `done` — silent while `B` is in backlog (breakdown not started), still firing as its own gate runs.
- **Plan-review enforces placement + link.** The plan-review prompt confirms the plan exists at the instructed location and is linked, since structural validation no longer blocks before the plan is due.

## Success Criteria

- [hard] REQ-01: After `jit apply <template> <container>` succeeds, `jit validate` passes with no errors from the not-yet-authored plan or from coverage of an un-started breakdown; reaching a valid state requires no manual file-creation step.
- [hard] REQ-02: Apply attaches no plan document reference. The planning node's description carries the resolved plan-doc location as an author-and-link instruction, and a `--force` refresh re-seeds that prose without creating a reference.
- [hard] REQ-03: A missing external plan is tolerated while the bracket's planning node is not `done`, and surfaces as an error once it is `done`; any other read failure (unreadable, parse) always errors.
- [hard] REQ-04: `coverage-preview` produces no finding while the breakdown node is in backlog, and enforces once it is `in_progress`, `gated`, or `done`.
- [hard] REQ-05: `plan-review` verifies the plan is present at the instructed location and linked to the planning node.
- [hard] REQ-06: Regression tests cover the apply-then-validate path (no link created; missing plan before/after planning is done) and the coverage state-gate (silent in backlog, enforcing in progress).

## Non-goals

- Changing the bracket graph shape or the set of nodes `jit apply` creates.
- Authoring real plan content — the planning phase produces and links the plan.
- A cross-repo migration pass; go-forward only, with the one live bracket reconciled by hand.

documents.label: Solution plan: jit apply plan-doc fix
